title: lathyrus ochraceus subsp. hispanicus
intro: Lathyrus ochraceus subsp. hispanicus, also known as the Spanish pease or vetchling, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic, and is used to treat urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its high concentration of oxalates and other compounds. The health benefits of Lathyrus ochraceus subsp. hispanicus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
